title: Woodsia euporolepis
taxonomy: Woodsia euporolepis is a species of fern in the family Woodsiaceae. It belongs to the genus Woodsia and is a type of lacy wood fern. The classification of Woodsia euporolepis is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Phylum: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Polypodiales, Family: Woodsiaceae, Genus: Woodsia, Species: W. euporolepis. Woodsia euporolepis is native to Asia and is known for its delicate and lacy fronds. This species is often found in mountainous regions and is highly prized for its ornamental value.

intro: Woodsia euporolepis, also known as the alpine woodsia, is a species of fern that belongs to the genus Woodsia and is native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant properties.
